senses.definition: to verbally or physically attack someone or something when one is angry about someone or something else

examples.x: I listened patiently while she vented her spleen.
Jack vented his spleen at his wife whenever things went badly at work.
Mom's been venting her spleen to me for an hour already.
Peter vented his spleen on his car by kicking it when it broke down.

senses.definition: to become greatly annoyed or angry

examples.x: She often loses her head when faced with unexpected challenges, making it difficult to find a solution.
He lost his head and said things he regretted in the heat of the argument.
The CEO lost her head and made impulsive decisions that negatively impacted the company's reputation.
I'm afraid she will lose her head when she receives the news, as she tends to react impulsively.
If we don't prepare well, we might lose our heads during the intense competition and make costly mistakes.

senses.definition: to become extremely angry with someone or something

examples.x: She hits the roof every time her siblings borrow her clothes without asking.
The boss hits the ceiling when employees consistently miss deadlines.
He hit the roof when he discovered his car had been scratched in the parking lot.
My parents hit the roof when they found out I had failed a class.
I am worried that she will hit the ceiling when she learns about the surprise party we planned for her.

senses.definition: to react to something in a very angry and furious way

examples.x: She goes up the wall every time her computer freezes in the middle of an important task.
I went up the wall when I realized I had lost my keys right before leaving for work.
They went up the wall after waiting for hours for their delayed flight to take off.
He will go up the wall if he finds out someone ate the last slice of his favorite pizza.

senses.definition: to lose one's temper or become really upset

examples.x: She goes spare whenever someone interrupts her while she's concentrating on a task.
The customer goes spare when the store runs out of their favorite product.
He went spare after discovering his car had been scratched in the parking lot.
My parents went spare when they found out I had failed my final exams.
I'm afraid she will go spare if she doesn't get the promotion she's been expecting.

senses.definition: to suddenly and violently express one's anger

examples.x: She goes off the deep end whenever someone criticizes her work.
The politician goes off the deep end when confronted with tough questions.
He went off the deep end after his favorite team lost the championship.
They went off the deep end when they discovered their project had been canceled.
I fear she will go off the deep end if she doesn't get the job she's been hoping for.
If they don't resolve the issue soon, the employees will go off the deep end and start protesting.

word_list.pronunciation: blˈoʊ wˈʌnz tˈɑːp kˈuːl stˈæk lˈɪd

senses.definition: to fail to control one's anger

examples.x: She blows her top whenever someone questions her authority.
The customer blew his top when he received a faulty product.
He blew his top when he found out his car had been towed.
They blew their top after waiting for hours for a service that never arrived.
I'm afraid she will blow her top if she doesn't get a satisfactory explanation.
If they continue to ignore his concerns, he will blow his top during the meeting.

senses.definition: to become crazy and completely lose control of one's thoughts and emotions

examples.x: She is losing her marbles and often forgets where she puts her keys.
The stress of the job is causing him to lose his marbles, and he's making irrational decisions.
He lost his marbles in his old age and started behaving erratically.
They lost their marbles during the difficult times and made questionable choices.

senses.definition: mentally confused and incapable of behaving in a reasonable way

examples.x: The constant noise from construction work might drive her around the bend.

senses.definition: to act in a very wild or extreme manner, often showing intense anger, excitement, or joy

examples.x: He goes nuts whenever someone questions his authority.
The customer went nuts after receiving poor customer service.
She went nuts when she found out about the betrayal.
They went nuts during the argument and said hurtful things.
If they keep ignoring his concerns, he will go nuts during the meeting.

word_list.word: like a red (flag|rag) to a bull
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪk ɐ ɹˈɛd flˈæɡ ɹˈæɡ tʊ ɐ bˈʊl

senses.definition: used to refer to something that will surely make someone angry

examples.x: The disrespectful comment was like a red flag to a bull, and he lost his temper.
The sight of the rival team's jersey was like a red rag to a bull, leading to a fierce confrontation.
Mentioning politics is like a red flag to a bull for him; he gets extremely heated and argumentative.
Loud noises are like a red rag to a bull for her, causing her to become agitated and anxious.

senses.definition: used to refer to a state in which someone is extremely nervous and unable to relax

examples.x: She is on edge before her big presentation.
He is on edge due to the stressful situation at work.
He will be on edge until he receives the test results.
We will be on edge until we hear back from the potential employer.

senses.definition: a situation or discussion that is filled with intense emotions, arguments, or conflict and lacks clear or useful information

examples.x: The debate between the two politicians is generating more heat than light, with personal attacks overshadowing actual policy discussions.
The team meeting became unproductive as people started arguing without offering constructive solutions, resulting in more heat than light.
The negotiations last week produced more heat than light, with both sides refusing to compromise and making little progress.
The panel discussion at the conference ended up generating more heat than light due to the constant interruptions and lack of focused discussion.
If they continue to approach the issue with hostility, the conversation is bound to generate more heat than light, hindering any possibility of finding a resolution.
Without a skilled mediator, the upcoming meeting has the potential to devolve into more heat than light, with participants becoming defensive and uncooperative.

senses.definition: a tendency to quickly lose one's temper

examples.x: She has a quick temper and often loses her composure during arguments.
He frequently displays a quick temper, especially when things don't go his way.
Yesterday, her quick temper got the best of her, and she ended up shouting at her coworkers.
In high school, he had a quick temper and would often get into fights with his peers.
If she doesn't work on managing her quick temper, it may negatively impact her relationships.
